questions.question: What will be the output of: console.log([] + [])?
questions.explanation: Both arrays are converted to empty strings, resulting in ''.

questions.question: What is the output of: typeof NaN?
questions.explanation: NaN is considered a number type in JavaScript.

questions.question: Which method is used to stop event bubbling?
questions.explanation: It prevents the event from bubbling up the DOM tree.

questions.question: What does useMemo return?
questions.explanation: useMemo caches the result of a computation.

questions.question:
What will be the output?

Promise.resolve(1).then(x => x + 1).then(console.log);
questions.explanation: The value is passed through the promise chain and incremented.

questions.question: Which hook is used to persist values across renders without causing re-render?
questions.explanation: useRef holds mutable values without triggering re-renders.

questions.question: What is the purpose of key in React lists?
questions.explanation: Keys help React identify which items have changed.

questions.question:
What will be the output?

console.log(0.1 + 0.2 === 0.3)
questions.explanation: Floating point precision causes slight inaccuracies.

questions.question: What is lazy loading in React?
questions.explanation: Lazy loading improves performance by splitting bundles.

questions.question: Which lifecycle is replaced by useEffect in functional components?
questions.explanation: useEffect can handle all these lifecycle phases.
